mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS signals rising household income and lower recession risk, causing investors to raise expected corporate revenues and bid up the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not produce a positive 20-day forward return spread versus lower growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in UNRATE signals improving labor-market slack and stronger consumption capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the move is not accompanied by immediate monetary tightening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:2014-05-15 snapshot UNRATE was 6.3 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases, or if their mean return is not above the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with subdued CPIAUCSL growth and near-zero FEDFUNDS identifies a goldilocks production expansion in which earnings expectations improve without a discount-rate shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive, CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is below 3%, and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between INDPRO growth and low CPIAUCSL growth has a non-positive coefficient for 60-day returns in walk-forward regressions, or if triggered returns underperform baseline by more than 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: An upside acceleration in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains near zero raises the probability of future policy tightening and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:2014-05-15 snapshot CPIAUCSL was 236.254 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if CPI acceleration at low FEDFUNDS has a positive walk-forward coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in RSAFS signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incorporate the demand surprise.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-05-15 snapshot RSAFS was 434571 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward S&P 500 returns following above-median 3-month RSAFS growth are not higher than returns following below-median growth in at least two thirds of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and lower cyclical credit creation, reducing expected growth for construction, durable goods, and financials and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2014-05-15 snapshot HOUST was 946 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return exceeds the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth crosses into contraction, firms face weaker utilization and operating leverage, causing downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction signals do not precede negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal hit rate for negative returns is below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in GDPC1 together with rising PAYEMS identifies broad real-demand expansion, increasing expected earnings and risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:2014-05-15 snapshot GDPC1 was 15946.6 and PAYEMS was 138252 thousand
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and PAYEMS condition fails to outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 50 basis points on average in walk-forward tests.
